call_list.title: UHHS <> Innovaccer | Demonstration
call_list.attendees: Lena Abdul-aziz, Sara Bonenfant, Kessler Brown, Jimmy Bresee III, Richard Romaniszyn, Keg Avakian
call_list.summary:
The meeting was centered around discussing medication adherence solutions and exploring potential collaborations to enhance healthcare operations.

Topics Discussed:
Medication Adherence Challenges
Richard Romaniszyn highlighted the bottleneck in managing medication adherence with only two dedicated
call_list.pain_point:
Limited staffing: only two dedicated FTE pharmacists to support over 500,000 patients, causing bottlenecks.
Population health efforts lagging behind due to staffing constraints.
Existing Epic system not triggering many needed alerts for medication adherence and quality measures.
Heavy reliance on PBM and Surescripts data, which have data refresh and encounter trigger limitations.
Manual, Excel-based tracking of patients leading to inefficiencies.
Difficulty in stratifying high-risk patients and preventing redundant outreach.
High percentage (60-70%) of patient calls result in no engagement, wasting pharmacist time.
Concern about potential overload if AI agents generate too many patient callbacks for limited staff.
Challenges with scheduling appointments automatically due to provider resistance.
Need for better integration and documentation to avoid double documentation in EMR.
Data refresh frequency limitations, especially with claims data, impacting real-time updates.
Uncertainty about direct connections to PBMs and data sources.
Need to balance AI automation with human oversight to handle complex cases.

call_list.title: PQS / HCSC
call_list.attendees: Emily Endres, Lin, Tracy T (CTR), Austin Albaugh, Mitch_weishaar, Jeffrey Tunney, Alen Pajazetovic, Joanne_wilson
call_list.summary:
The meeting was centered around introductions and discussions on pharmacy engagement strategies, particularly focusing on the LAMP program and its integration with health plans.

Topics Discussed:
Introductions
Emily Endres introduced herself as the Vice President of Pharmacy at PQS, along with Aust
call_list.pain_point:
Buyer mentions ongoing challenges with integration post-transaction, specifically IT infrastructure and system integration.
Difficulty in setting up direct vendor relationships due to complex IT and claims data exchange requirements.
Concerns about relying on PBMs adding margins to products, making direct connections preferable but harder to implement.
Pharmacy engagement varies, with some pharmacies less engaged and lacking clinical confidence to perform interventions.
Challenges in targeting and engaging the right pharmacies to maximize results within budget constraints.
Variability in pharmacy engagement and effectiveness, with some chains performing better than others.
The need to manage patient behavior change and pharmacy behavior change over time, with some patients difficult to engage or who change plans.
Lack of clinical confidence among pharmacy staff is a barrier to effective intervention delivery.

call_list.title: Internal PREP - UHHS Demo | Med Adherence and SUPD
call_list.attendees: Jill Marie Shewmake, Emily Endres, Jennifer Lynn Ray, Kessler Brown, Jimmy Bresee III, Keg Avakian
call_list.summary:
The meeting centered around discussing medication adherence measures and the preparation for an upcoming demo related to pharmacy operations and quality management.

Topics Discussed:
New Role and Responsibilities
Jennifer Lynn Ray expressed excitement about her new role, which involves engaging wit
call_list.pain_point:
Buyer is overwhelmed with sales calls, being pulled into calls they shouldn't be on, impacting their ability to focus on product and design work.
The organization has had trouble with data integration capabilities and standing up the medication adherence process in Epic.
The demo system lacks some current medication adherence measures and exclusions, requiring updates or custom builds.
There is confusion or lack of clarity about roles and ticket assignments, e.g., quality management team member Ashley was not assigned a ticket and lacks knowledge of the demo.
The buyer's team does not currently have a demo for the Part D medication adherence measure (S upd).
The demo system may be basic and not sophisticated enough for pharmacy users, raising concerns about appearing amateurish.
Right back functionality is down, limiting the ability to show longitudinal documentation records.
There is a need to clarify which team members should be involved in the demo and their roles.
The buyer's team is missing some pharmacy claims data in the demo system, affecting measure calculation accuracy.

call_list.title: Jessica Abraham( Keck Hospital of USC) & Innovaccer Connect
call_list.opportunity: USC_PQS, Keck Medicine Of USC_PQS, Keck Medicine Of USC_Pharmacy Network_Atlas

call_list.summary:
The meeting primarily focused on discussing pharmacy programs and potential collaborations to enhance medication adherence and healthcare services.

Topics Discussed:
Time Management and Travel Plans
Participants discussed how quickly time is passing and shared their travel plans, including work tri
call_list.pain_point:
Buyer notes that pharmacies are not fully tapping into available programs despite having access to Equip.
Pharmacies face time constraints and staffing challenges, making it difficult to justify participation in programs.
Some pharmacies have left networks due to insufficient financial incentives for additional work.
Lack of clarity and understanding about program fees and reimbursement structures among pharmacies.
Difficulty in obtaining eligibility and claims data directly from pharmacies; reliance on health plans for data.
Challenges in coordinating data flow and documentation requirements for program participation.
Telehealth services have limitations in meeting certain program documentation and reimbursement criteria.
Need to minimize additional effort for pharmacy staff in managing program data and outreach.
Concerns about the cost impact of program fees on pharmacies' budgets.
Uncertainty about how to integrate pharmacy dispensing data with clinical program requirements.

call_list.title: Stripe & Salesforce Integration Connect
call_list.attendees: Samrat Mehta, Manu Naik, Rishabh Garg, Udit Bansal
call_list.summary:
The meeting centered around the integration of various systems for better tracking and management of healthcare and pharmacy data.

Topics Discussed:
Dashboard and Integration Requirements
Discussion on creating a dashboard for healthcare tracking.
Integration of accounts and contacts into Salesforc
call_list.pain_point:
Seller asks: "Do we store the NPI number on Stripe as well?"
Buyer states: "The problem is matching data between Stripe and Salesforce, especially matching payments to NPIs."
Buyer states: "Invoices from Stripe do not contain NPI numbers, only number of NPIs purchased per email address."
Buyer states: "There is difficulty in tracking multi-store pharmacies because grouping data is incomplete or changes frequently."
Buyer states: "Challenge in identifying the primary contact person among multiple contacts associated with NPIs."
Buyer states: "Tracking payments and subscriptions accurately at the NPI level is complex due to data spread across Stripe, Mixpanel, and Salesforce."
Buyer states: "Need to split payments correctly among multiple NPIs under a parent pharmacy to get accurate sales data."
Buyer states: "Chain pharmacies are only tracked at chain level, not individual stores, limiting granularity."
Buyer states: "Data refresh and updates are needed monthly due to pharmacies moving between independent and chain status."
Buyer states: "Sales team needs to chase unpaid NPIs but lacks clear grouping and contact ownership information."
Buyer states: "Mixpanel currently lacks subscription type and dollar value data, limiting reporting capabilities."

call_list.title: Innovaccer Med Reconciliation and Adherance
call_list.opportunity: Ascension Health_Post Acute Analytics, Ascension Health_Sara for Provider, Ascension Health_IntelliH, Ascension - Population Health Platform, Ascension Health_Quality Reporting (eCQM/GPRO) for TX Market, Network Health - Risk Adjustment, Ascension_Pop Health_Co-Pilot, Ascension_Value_Provider, Ascension_Pre Production Environment, Ascension-Provider-Xccelerate Conference, Ascension _Mini-Xccelerate Conference, Ascension - Performance Incentives at POC, Ascension_Productivity_Provider, Ascension_Productivity_Flow - Coding

call_list.summary:
The meeting was centered around discussing the integration and capabilities of the Innovaccer platform, particularly focusing on medication management and care management functionalities.

Topics Discussed:
Technical Issues and Introductions
Initial technical difficulties with video and audio were a
call_list.pain_point:
Buyer indicates challenges with integrating internal programs such as Dispensary of Hope for referrals within the Innovator platform.
Buyer notes lack of ability currently to upload custom formularies (e.g., employee health plan formulary) into the product.
Buyer highlights the need for better linkage between medication adherence dashboards and patient-level care management workflows.
Buyer expresses need for filtering dashboards by region/state to ensure pharmacists only work within licensed areas.
Buyer points out current medication adherence data is received as spreadsheets from payers, which is a manual and cumbersome process.
Buyer notes that medication management dashboards and outreach capabilities are not yet live or fully integrated.
Buyer mentions disconnected EHR systems complicate data integration.
Buyer raises concern about the need for Surescripts data feed to cover Medicare Advantage patients adequately.
Buyer indicates that outreach workflows are varied and not fully streamlined across EMRs, Salesforce, and Innovator platform.

Topics Discussed:
Introduction and Background
David Kaskoun introduced Innovacer, highlighting its focus on
call_list.pain_point:
Buyer identified Pharmacy prior authorization as a major area needing evolution in 2026.
Buyer indicated challenges with siloed departments and disparate data sources.
Buyer and seller discussed issues with multiple chart requests for the same patient (up to 8-10 times).
Buyer concerned about integration with new Epic platform.
Seller noted medication adherence as a key area where current metrics are not as high as desired, especially for diabetes and hypertension.
Buyer acknowledged lowest ratings in certain contracts (e.g., D-SNP contract 83-79) leading to financial losses.
Buyer uncertain about organizational structure for quality and risk adjustment, indicating potential coordination challenges.

Topics Discussed:
Professional Updates
Jeev Sahni shared his career progression, including hi
call_list.pain_point:
Buyer acknowledges that Cerner's tools for data extraction are feasible but not user-friendly or presentable.
Buyer points out that current reporting for inventory and reordering is imperfect and requires manual calculations.
Buyer identifies the challenge of tracking thousands of items and needing a smarter system to recommend what and when to order.
Buyer notes the disconnect between consumption data (dispensed to patients) and stock issuance data, which complicates costing and management.
Buyer implies inefficiencies in current pharmacy and drug supply management processes due to fragmented data and lack of integration.

call_list.title: Innovaccer & INTEGRIS | Gravity & 340B Discussion

call_list.summary:
The meeting centered around the demonstration and discussion of the Gravity platform, focusing on its capabilities in supply chain management and data integration.

Topics Discussed:
Introduction and Setup
Initial greetings and technical setup issues were addressed.
Participants included David Fried
call_list.pain_point:
Buyer currently uses batch processing for data extracts, which is disliked.
Buyer does not want to build data extracts manually.
Buyer wants to avoid batch processing and move to API-generated data integration.
Buyer faces challenges in validating rebates, tracking denials, and managing resubmissions.
Buyer needs transparent, resilient supply chain tracking ("glass box model") to track vendors, payments, and accuracy.
Buyer has challenges with data quality and trust in analytics due to retrospective data governance.
Buyer team is used to cookie-cutter reporting and may find platform engagement advanced.
Buyer is concerned about change management and training for their team.
Buyer is unsure about metrics or number value gaps they want to close.

call_list.title: Jay manjunath GKHP
call_list.summary:
The meeting primarily revolved around discussions on travel experiences, work arrangements, and a detailed conversation about the Galaxy platform and its potential benefits.

Topics Discussed:
Travel and Personal Experiences
Michael Rime shared his recent travel experiences to Phoenix and San Franci
call_list.pain_point:
Seller asks about how layoffs affected the department: "How has that affected you and your department?"
Buyer states increased workload and challenges: "It's more of an 8-hour work day instead of a 12-hour workday. Now plus no vacation for a few months for me. I'm 18 and getting their weekly. Working on the weekends."
Buyer mentions managing automation is a big hassle: "Managing the automation is only a big, big hassle because we couldn't set up all the jobs, or I mean all the alerting system that we would like to hopefully end of the, in the next part of the quarter, we would be able to do it."
Buyer acknowledges gap reporting is late and inefficient: "Currently, we are a little bit late on the Gap reporting and also the gaps. Why don't we have a very efficient system?"
Buyer describes manual outreach processes: "For medication adherence, we have a pharmacy person, will pass that along to her and she will reach out manually to everyone."
Buyer indicates that gap reports and medication adherence reports built from scratch are not 100% correct: "We built it from scratch. It's still not 100% correct but just with two people or three people in the team."
Buyer mentions layoffs leading to a smaller team: "We laid off a lot of people. So it's like very small genome."
